envs.name: GITHUB_REPO_URL
envs.description: GitHub API repo URL for IVX (e.g. https://api.github.com/repos/<owner>/<repo>). Used to read the real main-branch SHA and push.

envs.name: GITHUB_TOKEN
envs.description: GitHub personal access token with contents:read/write:public_key on the IVX repo. Used to verify the real main SHA and confirm pushes.

envs.name: RENDER_API_KEY
envs.description: Render API key (Bearer token) to list deploys, trigger a new deploy, and read deploy status for service RENDER_SERVICE_ID.

envs.name: RENDER_SERVICE_ID
envs.description: Render service ID for the IVX backend (api.ivxholding.com). Used with RENDER_API_KEY to trigger and verify deploys.

envs.name: SUPABASE_URL
envs.description: Supabase project URL (https://<project>.supabase.co). Used to verify member/investor/transaction tables live.

envs.name: SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY
envs.description: Supabase service-role key. Used server-side to read/verify real member, investor, transaction, and audit rows.

envs.name: IVX_OWNER_EMAIL
envs.description: Owner account email used to authenticate against the live backend for the authenticated smoke test.

envs.name: IVX_OWNER_PASSWORD
envs.description: Owner account password used to obtain an access token for the authenticated live smoke test.

envs.name: IVX_OWNER_TOKEN
envs.description: Existing owner JWT/Supabase access token (if you already have one). Used to call owner-protected routes live.

To honestly finish this (push a real commit, trigger Render, verify the new SHA live, and run an authenticated smoke test against Supabase) I need them actually injected into my environment. Without them I can verify the backend is up but cannot produce a real GitHub SHA + Render Deploy ID + authenticated proof chain.

requestedEnvs.description: Supabase service-role key. Used server-side to read/verify real member, investor, transaction, and audit rows.

requestedEnvs.name: IVX_OWNER_EMAIL
requestedEnvs.description: Owner account email used to authenticate against the live backend for the authenticated smoke test.

requestedEnvs.name: IVX_OWNER_PASSWORD
requestedEnvs.description: Owner account password used to obtain an access token for the authenticated live smoke test.

requestedEnvs.name: IVX_OWNER_TOKEN
requestedEnvs.description: Existing owner JWT/Supabase access token (if you already have one). Used to call owner-protected routes live.

output.reason: I ran `env` in my shell and the IVX credentials (GITHUB_TOKEN, RENDER_API_KEY, SUPABASE_URL, IVX_OWNER_*) are all empty — only SUPABASE_CLI_VERSION is set. To honestly finish this (push a real commit, trigger Render, verify the new SHA live, and run an authenticated smoke test against Supabase) I need them actually injected into my environment. Without them I can verify the backend is up but cannot produce a real GitHub SHA + Render Deploy ID + authenticated proof chain.
